summary.confidence_reason: The score is backed by a broad set of applicable page-level checks.

measured: page-level title, description, headings, links, images, and assets
robots.txt and sitemap as supporting site context
Google and Yandex page-level metadata and markup heuristics
not_measured: real browser-rendered DOM after JavaScript execution
Core Web Vitals field data and lab metrics
paid backlink, SERP, or competitor datasets

semantics.first_paragraph: This domain is for use in documentation examples without needing permission. Avoid use in operations.

blockers.title: The audited page has strong crawl guidance for Yandex
blockers.recommendation: Publish robots.txt, expose a valid XML sitemap, and reference the sitemap from robots.txt.

blockers.title: The audited page exposes markup Yandex can interpret
blockers.recommendation: Add structured metadata where relevant and validate it in Yandex Webmaster tooling.

blockers.title: The audited page has snippet signals that are usable for Yandex results
blockers.recommendation: Keep a specific title and a clear meta description in practical snippet ranges so Yandex can form stronger result previews.

strengths: The audited page opens with an answer-friendly introduction for AI systems
The audited page is easy to summarize into a clear AI answer

missing.title: The audited page includes question-led sections that map to conversational intent
missing.recommendation: Turn key subtopics into explicit question-style headings such as what, how, why, pricing, process, or FAQ-style prompts.

missing.title: The audited page clearly identifies the entity behind the content
missing.recommendation: Expose clearer organization, service, person, about-page, and contact signals so the entity behind the content is easier to identify.

missing.title: The audited page is structured into reusable chunks for AI summarization
missing.recommendation: Break the page into clearer sections with descriptive H2/H3 headings, short answer blocks, lists, and comparison tables where relevant.

missing.title: The audited page exposes schema types that help AI systems understand entities and answers
missing.recommendation: Add the schema types that match the visible page purpose, such as Service, Person, FAQPage, HowTo, WebPage, or WebSite.

geo_priorities.title: Expand machine-readable GEO schema
geo_priorities.why: Richer page-, service-, person-, FAQ-, and website-level schema helps AI systems identify entities and reusable answer structures.
geo_priorities.fix_now: Add the schema types that match the visible page purpose, especially Service, Person, FAQPage, HowTo, WebPage, or WebSite.

geo_priorities.title: Break the page into cleaner answer blocks
geo_priorities.why: AI systems summarize better when pages have explicit sections, lists, and reusable chunks.
geo_priorities.fix_now: Use clearer H2/H3 sections, add lists or tables for comparisons, and separate key points into smaller blocks.

lead_trust_priorities.title: Make contacting you effortless
lead_trust_priorities.why: Weak contact visibility reduces conversion from high-intent visitors.
lead_trust_priorities.fix_now: Show a preferred contact method near the offer and repeat it lower on the page.

lead_trust_priorities.title: Show one obvious next step
lead_trust_priorities.why: Users need a simple visible action before they turn into leads.
lead_trust_priorities.fix_now: Add one primary CTA near the top such as request a call, get pricing, or send an inquiry.

lead_trust_priorities.title: Add proof near the offer
lead_trust_priorities.why: Trust proof often decides whether a visitor contacts you now or continues comparing options.
lead_trust_priorities.fix_now: Place reviews, case studies, guarantees, certificates, or portfolio proof beside the main promise and CTA.

prioritized_actions.title: The audited page exposes a charset declaration
prioritized_actions.details: The audited page does not expose a charset declaration in the parsed HTML.
prioritized_actions.recommendation: Declare a charset such as UTF-8 in the HTML head.

prioritized_actions.title: The audited page title length stays in a practical snippet range
prioritized_actions.details: The title looks shorter or longer than the typical practical range for search snippets.
prioritized_actions.recommendation: Keep the title specific and usually within roughly 30-65 characters while prioritizing intent clarity.

prioritized_actions.title: The audited page exposes a meta description
prioritized_actions.details: The audited page does not expose a meta description.
prioritized_actions.recommendation: Add a concise, intent-matching description that summarizes page value clearly.

prioritized_actions.title: The audited page contains enough visible copy to express topical depth
prioritized_actions.details: The audited page provides very little visible copy, which may limit topical clarity.
prioritized_actions.recommendation: Expand the page copy so it answers the target intent more completely.

prioritized_actions.title: The audited page has a healthy main-content-to-template ratio
prioritized_actions.details: A large share of visible page text appears to come from template or surrounding boilerplate.
prioritized_actions.recommendation: Strengthen the main content block and reduce repetitive template text around the core page topic.

prioritized_actions.title: The audited page avoids generic anchor text
prioritized_actions.details: Some links use generic anchors that weaken semantic clarity for users and search engines.
prioritized_actions.recommendation: Replace generic anchors with destination-specific phrases that describe the next page more clearly.

prioritized_actions.title: The audited page exposes Open Graph metadata
prioritized_actions.details: Open Graph metadata is missing on the audited page.
prioritized_actions.recommendation: Add Open Graph tags so the page previews well in messengers and social platforms.

prioritized_actions.title: The audited page exposes Twitter Card metadata
prioritized_actions.details: Twitter Card metadata is missing on the audited page.
prioritized_actions.recommendation: Add Twitter Card metadata so link previews remain consistent outside of search results.

issues.title: The audited page has strong crawl guidance for Yandex
issues.details: The audited page lacks both robots.txt and a valid sitemap, which weakens crawl discovery and canonical guidance for Yandex.

issues.title: The audited page has snippet signals that are usable for Yandex results
